2604.09497.title: BERT-as-a-Judge: A Robust Alternative to Lexical Methods for Efficient Reference-Based LLM Evaluation
2604.09497.paper_summary: Accurate evaluation is central to the large language model (LLM) ecosystem, guiding model selection and downstream adoption across diverse use cases. In practice, however, evaluating generative outputs typically relies on rigid lexical methods to extract and assess answers, which can conflate a model's true problem-solving ability with its compliance with predefined formatting guidelines. While recent LLM-as-a-Judge approaches mitigate this issue by assessing semantic correctness rather than strict structural conformity, they also introduce substantial computational overhead, making evaluation costly. In this work, we first systematically investigate the limitations of lexical evaluation through a large-scale empirical study spanning 36 models and 15 downstream tasks, demonstrating that such methods correlate poorly with human judgments. To address this limitation, we introduce BERT-as-a-Judge, an encoder-driven approach for assessing answer correctness in reference-based generative settings, robust to variations in output phrasing, and requiring only lightweight training on synthetically annotated question-candidate-reference triplets. We show that it consistently outperforms the lexical baseline while matching the performance of much larger LLM judges, providing a compelling tradeoff between the two and enabling reliable, scalable evaluation. Finally, through extensive experimentation, we provide detailed insights into BERT-as-a-Judge's performance to offer practical guidance for practitioners, and release all project artifacts to foster downstream adoption.
2604.09497.paper_summary_zh: 準確的評估對於大型語言模型（LLM）生態系統至關重要，指導模型選擇和在各種使用案例中的下游採用。<lb/>然而，在實踐中，評估生成的輸出通常依賴於僵化的詞彙方法來提取和評估答案，這可能會將模型的真正解決問題能力與其遵循預定格式指導方針的程度混淆。<lb/>雖然最近的LLM作為裁判的方法通過評估語義正確性而非嚴格的結構一致性來減輕這一問題，但它們也引入了相當大的計算開銷，使得評估變得昂貴。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們首先通過一項涵蓋36個模型和15個下游任務的大規模實證研究系統性地調查了詞彙評估的局限性，顯示這些方法與人類判斷的相關性很差。<lb/>為了解決這一局限性，我們引入了BERT作為裁判，這是一種基於編碼器的方法，用於在基於參考的生成設置中評估答案的正確性，對輸出措辭的變化具有魯棒性，並且只需在合成標註的問題-候選-參考三元組上進行輕量級訓練。<lb/>我們展示了它在性能上始終超越詞彙基準，同時與更大規模的LLM裁判的性能相匹配，提供了兩者之間引人注目的權衡，並實現了可靠、可擴展的評估。<lb/>最後，通過大量實驗，我們提供了有關BERT作為裁判性能的詳細見解，以為從業者提供實用指導，並釋放所有項目文檔以促進下游採用。
2604.09497.author: Hippolyte Gisserot-Boukhlef et.al.
2604.09497.authors: Hippolyte Gisserot-Boukhlef, Nicolas Boizard, Emmanuel Malherbe, Céline Hudelot, Pierre Colombo
